allies.rationale_text: Scientific-method-evidence-testing gives bayesian reasoning a disciplined way to generate hypotheses and update beliefs with empirical data.
allies.source_quote: The deliberative structure of Bayesianism aligns directly with the scientific method, emphasizing testable hypotheses and empirical data gathering.

allies.rationale_text: Base-rates stabilizes bayesian reasoning by supplying the outside-view prior before case-specific evidence is layered in.
allies.source_quote: To provide an objective outside view, one must anchor initial estimates to reference classes (what has happened in similar past situations).

allies.rationale_text: Expected-value pairs with bayesian reasoning by turning updated probabilities into better decision stakes under uncertainty.
allies.source_quote: It underpins all optimal decision-making under uncertainty.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias blocks bayesian updating by filtering evidence so the posterior never honestly moves.
antagonists.source_quote: Bayesianism's explicit requirement to incorporate disconfirming evidence to update the prior belief fundamentally clashes with this tendency.

antagonists.rationale_text: Anchoring distorts bayesian reasoning when the initial prior keeps too much weight after contradictory evidence arrives.
antagonists.source_quote: Anchoring occurs when too much focus is placed on a single, often initial, piece of information.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Bayesian conflicts with confirmation-bias when posterior updates are blocked by selective evidence acceptance.
structured_tensions.source_quote: bayesian vs confirmation-bias: bayesian conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when posterior updates are blocked by selective evidence acceptance.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Bayesian conflicts with representativeness-heuristic when intuitive resemblance overrides probability revision.
structured_tensions.source_quote: bayesian vs representativeness-heuristic: bayesian conflicts with representativeness-heuristic (representativeness heuristic) when intuitive resemblance overrides probability revision.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strong on ally logic around scientific testing and outside-view priors, plus an explicit structured tension block.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation expansion beyond the named testing, prior, and anti-bias surface.
open_questions: Fast-thinking is source-real conflict, but confirmation-bias and anchoring were the cleaner bounded antagonists into the current graph vocabulary.
